title: Aerodynamics Engineer
description_text: About The Role: We are seeking an exceptional Aerodynamics Engineer to contribute to the design and analysis of our high super sonic vehicles. As an Aerodynamics Engineer, you will play a crucial role in the development of advanced aerodynamic solutions, ensuring optimal performance, stability, and efficiency during high-speed flight. Join our dynamic team and be part of the journey to create groundbreaking aerospace solutions at an iterative pace not seen since the 60's.

title: Avionics Electrical Engineer
description_text: As an Avionics Electrical Engineer at Hermeus, you will be responsible for the design, testing, and integration of GNC systems, electrical hardware, control software, power distribution, and RF communications for our hypersonic vehicles.

title: Avionics Electrical Engineer
description_text: As an Avionics Electrical Engineer at Hermeus, you will be responsible for the design, testing, and integration of GNC systems, electrical hardware, control software, power distribution, and RF communications for our hypersonic vehicles.

title: Avionics Electrical Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: As an Avionics Engineering Intern you will help shape the future of commercial supersonic and hypersonic flight. Our team is responsible for the design, testing, and integration of GNC systems, electrical hardware, control software, power distribution, and RF communications.   Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Avionics Manufacturing Engineer
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.

title: Avionics Shop Manager
description_text: As a Manager of the Avionics Build you will have a unique opportunity to build and lead a cross-functional team of individuals that develop wire harnessing and integrated electro-mechanical assemblies.  You will be responsible for standing up and optimizing end-to-end avionics harnessing and electro-mechanical assembly capabilities that ensure high-speed, high-reliability, manufacturing with a commitment to cost reduction, novel process definition, and rapid iteration.

title: Avionics Systems Engineer – Flight Safety Systems (FTS / AFSS)
description_text: As an Avionics Systems Engineer, you will be responsible for owning the design, integration, testing, and certification of the Flight Termination System (FTS) hardware and software for advanced flight vehicles. You will work closely with cross-functional teams across avionics, electrical, mechanical, and ground systems engineering to ensure seamless system integration and compliance with range safety requirements. In this role, you will coordinate with regulatory authorities, manage system interfaces, and support overall vehicle readiness through development, testing, and flight operations. You will also serve as the technical authority for all Flight Termination System-related activities, ensuring system reliability, documentation, and mission success.

title: Build Engineer
description_text: This role sits at the intersection of engineering design, manufacturing, integration and test for flight vehicles. You'll be embedded with design and production teams, owning everything from hardware design, tooling, testing and flight readiness to process development and vehicle assembly. We are hiring entry-level through senior-level.

title: Build Engineer - Propulsion
description_text: This role sits at the intersection of engineering design, manufacturing, integration and test for flight vehicles. You'll be embedded with design and production teams, owning everything from hardware design, tooling, testing and flight readiness to process development and vehicle assembly.

title: Build Engineer - Structures
description_text: This role sits at the intersection of engineering design, manufacturing, integration and test for flight vehicles. You'll be embedded with design and production teams, owning everything from hardware design, tooling, testing and flight readiness to process development and vehicle assembly. We are hiring entry-level through senior-level.

title: Build Engineer - Tooling
description_text: A Tooling Build Engineer on the Manufacturing team is responsible for the design, development, and validation of innovative tooling, fixtures, and ground support equipment that enable the prototyping, integration, and testing of advanced structural assemblies. This role emphasizes creativity, rapid iteration, and collaboration with multidisciplinary teams to support early-stage research and development.

title: Business Applications Developer
description_text: Hermeus is actively seeking a passionate and talented individual to join our expanding team as a Business Applications Developer. As a pivotal member, you will play a key role in designing, developing, enhancing, and maintaining integrations and customizations across our ERP, MES and PLM applications. This is a unique opportunity to work on mission-critical systems that connect Engineering, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Finance, enabling efficient data flow and process automation across the company.  While experience with specific platforms such as NetSuite and Manufacturo is a plus, we value strong development skills, integration experience, and the ability to quickly learn new frameworks and technologies. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to deliver scalable technical solutions that align with business needs and organizational objectives.

title: Chief Engineer - Airbreathing Propulsion Test
description_text: We are seeking a 1st principles driven, experienced, and owner mindset Chief Engineer to lead the design, construction, and activation of an expansion at our next-generation airbreathing propulsion test facility, HEAT , located in Jacksonville, FL. This facility expansion will play a critical role in advancing hypersonic and other advanced propulsion technologies vital to Hermeus’ roadmap, national security, and aerospace innovation. As the Chief Engineer, you will serve as the technical authority and strategic lead responsible for the end-to-end development of the test facility—from concept and requirements definition through commissioning and operational readiness. This role will require close collaboration with internal teams, external contractors, and DoD stakeholders to ensure that the facility meets current and future propulsion testing needs.

title: Components Manufacturing Engineer
description_text: We are seeking a Components Manufacturing Engineer to be the first of its kind at our facility — responsible for establishing, developing, and scaling advanced manufacturing processes for critical hardware. You’ll take full ownership of component fabrication from concept through production, driving design-for-manufacture, process development, and continuous improvement across a wide range of precision mechanical systems.    This role requires a highly technical, hands-on engineer with strong problem-solving skills and the ability to operate autonomously in a fast-paced, first-principles environment. You’ll work closely with design, materials, and production teams to bridge the gap between prototype and production, helping define what world-class manufacturing looks like for our next-generation hardware.

title: Components Manufacturing Engineer - Sheet Metal
description_text: We are seeking a Sheet Metal Manufacturing Engineer to design, build, and lead the processes required to establish and operate our internal sheet metal fabrication shop. You will take full ownership of standing up new production capabilities, from design feedback and process definition to equipment procurement, qualification, and production ramp-up.    This role is highly hands-on, combining deep technical expertise with operational leadership to enable rapid, high-quality production of precision components supporting next-generation hypersonic aircraft.

title: Day Porter / Janitor – Manufacturing Support (Mid-Shift)
description_text: We are seeking a dependable and detail-oriented Day Porter/Janitor to support our manufacturing floor operations. This role is responsible for maintaining a clean, safe, and organized environment throughout the facility, with a strong focus on the production floor. The position will also provide backup support to the current Day Porter as needed. The ideal candidate is proactive, reliable, and able to work independently in a fast-paced manufacturing setting.

title: DevOps Engineer – Flight Software
description_text: Hermeus is a venture-backed defense aviation company reclaiming the lost art of rapid iterative prototyping to build the fastest aircraft in the world today. By prioritizing relentless hardware iteration, we deliver high-speed systems at the pace of the modern battlefield. We work with the Department of War to provide the high-speed capabilities our nation and its allies need to maintain a durable, asymmetric advantage.

title: Director, Software Engineering
description_text: As the Director of Software Engineering at Hermeus, you will be responsible for overseeing the software engineering function for our high-speed vehicles. Your primary focus will be on leading the design, development, and integration of software systems that enable safe and efficient operation of our high-speed aircraft. You will manage a talented team of software engineers and collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including avionics, vehicle engineering, and systems integration, to ensure seamless software integration throughout the aircraft.

title: Engineering Application Support Engineer - NX/Teamcenter
description_text: Hermeus is scaling its engineering systems following the deployment of Siemens NX and Teamcenter. As we move from go-live into stabilization and continuous improvement, we are building a dedicated capability to support engineering teams and ensure system reliability, usability, and performance. This is not a traditional help desk role. This position sits at the intersection of CAD, PLM, and engineering workflows, focused on diagnosing system issues, improving system behavior, and enabling engineers to move faster with confidence.     About The Role: As an  Engineering Application Support Engineer - NX/Teamcenter , you will be the frontline for engineering productivity - resolving issues, identifying systemic gaps, and driving continuous improvements across the NX and Teamcenter environment.

title: Engineering System Support Engineer
description_text: Hermeus is scaling its engineering systems following the deployment of Siemens NX and Teamcenter. As we move from go-live into stabilization and continuous improvement, we are building a dedicated capability to support engineering teams and ensure system reliability, usability, and performance. This is not a traditional help desk role. This position sits at the intersection of CAD, PLM, and engineering workflows, focused on diagnosing system issues, improving system behavior, and enabling engineers to move faster with confidence.     About The Role: As an Engineering System Support Engineer , you will be the frontline for engineering productivity - resolving issues, identifying systemic gaps, and driving continuous improvements across the NX and Teamcenter environment.

title: Executive Assistant
description_text: About The Role: As an Executive Assistant, you will effectively manage the wide range of time demands for a small group of senior executives at Hermeus. You’ll be empowered to own the executive’s calendar and help them more effectively manage when, where, and how they execute their duties. Your impact on the business is massive as you team with the business’ senior leaders to deliver the world’s fastest airplane.

title: F100 MRO
description_text: As an  MRO Propulsion Engineer you will take ownership of the m aintenance, refurbishment, and overhaul (MRO)  lifecycle of our F100 engines—including acceptance testing. This is a hands-on, high-impact role where you’ll work side-by-side with passionate engineers and technicians to keep our propulsion systems performing at their best. If you want to learn fast, solve hard problems, and contribute directly to hypersonic capability, this is the place for you.

title: Flight Dynamics Engineer
description_text: About The Role: Hermeus is seeking a Dynamics Engineer to join our growing Flight Sciences Team. In this role, you will perform analysis, modeling, and testingto verify subsystem dynamics and control system performance for our high-speed aircraft projects. As part of Hermeus’ iterative path to high-speed flight, you will drive lessons learned into follow-onaircraft requirements from the clean-sheet design phase through analysis, build, and flight test.

title: Flight Software Engineer
Flight Software Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: As a Flight Software Intern, you will support the design and implementation of mission systems for uncrewed high-speed aircraft. You will work with the A-GRA (OMS/UCI) framework to help develop interoperable systems and messaging architectures.   Summer: Around 12 weeks (May - August) Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Flight Software Test Verification & Validation (V&V) Engineer
description_text: Hermeus is a venture-backed defense aviation company reclaiming the lost art of rapid iterative prototyping to build the fastest aircraft in the world today. By prioritizing relentless hardware iteration, we deliver high-speed systems at the pace of the modern battlefield. We work with the Department of War to provide the high-speed capabilities our nation and its allies need to maintain a durable, asymmetric advantage.

title: Flight Test Technician
description_text: About The Role:  As a Flight Test Technician, you will be responsible for the testing and validation of aircraft systems, ensuring their performance and safety. This position will collaborate with engineers and pilots to execute flight test plans, collect data, and contribute to the development of cutting-edge aerospace technologies.

title: Fluids Integration Technician
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.   As a Fluids Integration Technician , you’ll be responsible for assembling, installing, and testing complex fluid and electromechanical systems in support of flight and propulsion hardware. You will work hands-on with hydraulic tubing, mechanical sub-assemblies, and pressurized systems while collaborating closely with engineering and operations teams. This role is ideal for a highly skilled technician who thrives in fast-paced environments, is fluent in reading technical schematics, and is passionate about building and improving flight-critical systems.

title: Fluids Manufacturing Engineer
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.

title: GNC Engineer - Navigation & System Integration
description_text: About The Role: The Navigation and System Integration GNC Engineer will be responsible for integration, testing, validation and verification of Navigation instrumentation and GNSS components and supporting the navigation SW suite and development efforts (including filtering and fusion) on advanced guidance, navigation and control systems for our aircraft, as needed.

title: GNC Engineering Intern (Controls) - Fall 2026
description_text: As a GNC Engineering Intern, you will support the development of guidance, navigation, and control systems for uncrewed high-speed aircraft. This role focuses heavily on controls development, algorithm implementation, and testing.   Summer: Around 12 weeks (May - August) Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Head of Supply Chain
description_text: We are seeking a strategic and systems-minded leader to build, own, and lead Hermeus' end-to-end Supply Chain function. You will define the strategy, build the organization, and establish integrated processes to support our ambitious roadmap; developing scalable, fit-for-purpose practices across the full lifecycle from advanced development to full-rate production.   Working directly with executive leadership, you'll translate company objectives into actionable supply chain strategies, building resilient supplier networks and disciplined processes that connect suppliers to shop floor with transparency and accountability. You'll ensure supply chain is a strategic enabler of Hermeus' mission.

title: HR Business Partner
description_text: About the Role:  As an HR Business Partner at Hermeus you will work closely with leaders to align people strategy with business goals. You will support team structure, performance management, and organizational planning while ensuring smooth HR operations and compliance at the Los Angeles site. This role balances strategic partnership with hands-on execution, driving efficiency, supporting growth, and creating an exceptional employee experience in a fast-paced aerospace environment.

title: HR Coordinator
description_text: About The Role: This role will work closely with recruiters, candidates, hiring managers, and cross-functional partners to create and deliver an outstanding experience for every candidate while also supporting key HR coordination efforts to ensure a seamless transition from offer acceptance through onboarding.

title: HR Generalist
description_text: About this Role   As an HR Generalist you will provide on-site People Operations support for the Los Angeles office. This role is responsible for executing core HR programs with a focus on employee relations, investigations, onboarding, and terminations. You will partners closely with People Ops leadership and site leaders to ensure consistent, compliant, and high-quality employee experiences.    This is an execution-focused role that requires strong judgment, discretion, and the ability to handle sensitive situations with professionalism.

title: HR Generalist
description_text: About this Role:  As an HR Generalist you will own and execute core People Operations processes across the employee lifecycle. You will be responsible for ensuring onboarding through offboarding runs smoothly, employee data is accurate and audit-ready, and cross-functional teams stay aligned and accountable.   This is a hands-on, operations-focused role for someone who can manage multiple workflows, solve problems in real time, and improve processes as they go.

title: Integration Engineer - Avionics
description_text: We are seeking an  Avionics   Integration Engineer  to support vehicle avionics hardware from design release through assembly, test, and final delivery.  In this role,  you will be a critical part of a cross-disciplinary team supporting aggressive development timelines for every electrical system on-vehicle.  This position influences design and system architecture trades based on manufacturability and performance during test.  You will also support standing up our internal AVI shop as we develop and qualify processes to produce harnesses,  build boxes,  and perform checkouts.

title: Integration Engineer – Fluid & Propulsion Systems
description_text: We are seeking an Integration Engineer to own the design release, build, test, and delivery of key vehicle fluid, propulsion, and mechanism assemblies — including control surface actuators, spike inlet systems, engine integration, and other mission-critical hardware.    You will serve as the technical bridge between design, manufacturing, and test, ensuring our hardware is built with precision, efficiency, and reliability. This role requires a hands-on engineer with a strong background in mechanical systems, propulsion, and fluids — capable of driving complex assemblies from concept through validation in a fast-paced, first-principles environment.

title: Integration Engineer – Structures & Assembly
description_text: We are seeking an Integration Engineer to take ownership of structural hardware from design release through build, test, and delivery — including airframe primary structure, wings, tail, control surfaces, and other critical load-bearing assemblies.    You’ll serve as the key link between design, manufacturing, and test — ensuring structural assemblies are built to exacting standards of precision, quality, and reliability. This is a highly hands-on, cross-functional role that combines deep technical understanding with strong program execution and first-principles problem-solving.

title: L&D Associate
description_text: About this Role:   As an L&D Associate you will support our learning initiatives end-to-end. You will help manage LMS administration, content creation and management, training coordination, facilitation support, and evaluation of training effectiveness. This role requires strong communication, attention to detail, and the ability to partner with team leaders to translate business needs into practical learning solutions.

title: Lead Avionics Engineer
description_text: We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Lead Avionics Engineer to join our dynamic team and contribute to the development of the next generation of hypersonic vehicles. As a Lead Avionics Engineer at Hermeus, you will play a pivotal role in leading the design, analysis, and testing of electrical systems for our hypersonic vehicles. You will collaborate closely with a multidisciplinary team of engineers to ensure the safe and efficient operation of our electrical systems. This position offers a unique opportunity to work on groundbreaking technology in a fast-paced and collaborative environment.

title: Lead Propulsion Component Engineer
description_text: As a Lead Propulsion Component Engineer you will own the development of flight-critical propulsion components—including valves, actuators, and high-performance mechanical systems operating in extreme thermal and pressure environments. This is a player–coach role: you will directly design and deliver hardware while leading a small team of engineers. You will own components from clean-sheet design through test, qualification, and flight, and your hardware will move quickly from concept to real-world operation.

title: Loads and Dynamics Engineer
description_text: About The Role: As a Loads and Dynamics Engineer you will support the development of our supersonic & hypersonic aircraft programs. This individual will work closely with the aerodynamics and structures teams to develop and refine external loads, create dynamic simulations, validate analysis through testing and flight, and evaluate flutter behaviors to support aircraft development.

title: Manufacturing Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: Hermeus is seeking talented students to join our Manufacture Engineering team in Atlanta.     Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Manufacturing Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: Hermeus is seeking talented students to join our Manufacture Engineering team in Los Angeles   to support improvement initiatives to expand in-house manufacturing capabilities and contribute to the design, build, and testing of flight and critical ground support hardware.   Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Manufacturing Systems Specialist
description_text: About The Role: As a  Manufacturing Systems Specialist, you  will become the internal authority for our Manufacturing Execution System(Manufacturo) and, over time, support the broader digital manufacturing ecosystem (MES  →  ERP  →  PLM). This is a  hands-on, highly cross-functional role   focused on enabling manufacturing operations through system support, workflow improvement, user training, configuration management, and process optimization. You will work directly with technicians, engineering, quality, inventory, and supply chain teams to ensure Manufacturo is scalable, intuitive, and operationally effective as the company continues to grow.

title: Mechanical Engineer
description_text: About The Role: We are seeking a Mid-Career Mechanical Engineer to support the design, build, and test of next-generation aircraft aerostructures, including fuselage, empennage, control surfaces, and landing gear systems. This role is hands-on and execution-oriented, with responsibility for taking hardware from concept through detailed design, fabrication support, and test validation.

title: Mechanical Engineering Intern  - Fall 2026
description_text: Hermeus is seeking talented students with experience in mechanical engineering to join our Fluids Team in Los Angeles. The Fluids Team is responsible for the design and integration of hydraulic systems, fuel systems, and their supporting structural components for cutting-edge hypersonic aircraft.   Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Mission Systems Platform Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: Hermeus is seeking a highly hands-on, curious, and resourceful Platform Intern to support our Mission Systems Engineering team. This role is ideal for a “builder”,  someone who enjoys working across hardware, software, networking, and physical systems, and isn’t afraid to pick up tools and figure things out. You’ll work at the intersection of IT infrastructure, flight systems, and real-world operations — supporting everything from ground control stations and networks to custom hardware setups and test environments.   Summer: Around 12 weeks (May - August) Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: NX Teamcenter PLM Support Engineer
description_text: About The Role: As an NX Teamcenter PLM Support Engineer, you will act as the frontline for engineering productivity - resolving issues, identifying systemic gaps, and driving continuous improvements across the NX and Teamcenter environment.  This position sits at the intersection of CAD, PLM, and engineering workflows, focused on diagnosing complex issues, improving system behavior, and enabling engineers to move faster with confidence.

title: Operational Analysis and Modeling Engineer
description_text: About The Role: We are currently seeking a creative and dynamic professional to build and lead operational analysis and simulation at Hermeus. As head of operational modeling and simulation, your work will directly shape the series of hypersonic aircraft Hermeus is developing as well as the capabilities they will bring to solve some of the most pressing challenges our customers face.  This role requires government, defense industry, and/or FFRDC/UARC experience and mastery of relevant software.  This role will be filled at a level commensurate with the applicant’s qualifications and experience either at the manager or director level.

title: Operations and Test Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: At Hermeus, we are constantly iterating and integrating. We test early and often to minimize technical and program risk. We build the test campaigns that support our full range of test programs spanning development, operational and certification testing.   Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Operations Supervisor
description_text: We are seeking an  Operations Supervisor  to lead all logistics execution in a fast-paced, hardware-focused startup environment as we stand up a new facility. This role owns material movement through multiple new factories as we bring them online; supports development and refinement of our MES, ERP, and other digital tools; and defining and iterating on the processes we use to deliver fast planes fast – literally.  This hands-on leadership position oversees the Production Coordination and Inventory teams across all Los Angeles-area factories.

title: OSRC Specialist
description_text: About the role:    As an OSRC Specialist you will play a critical role in supporting operational safety, risk reduction, compliance, and workplace readiness across manufacturing and production environments. This position partners closely with operations, facilities, engineering, and leadership teams to proactively identify risks, implement practical safety solutions, and help drive a strong culture of accountability and operational excellence.   This is a hands-on role requiring a strong field presence, sound judgment, technical problem-solving ability, and the capability to balance urgency with professionalism. The ideal candidate understands how to work collaboratively within fast-paced production environments while helping teams execute safely and efficiently.   The OSRC Specialist will support the organization’s mission by driving continuous improvement initiatives related to workplace safety, ergonomics, hazardous material management, operational readiness, and regulatory compliance.

title: Performance Engineer
description_text: About The Role: In your role as a Performance Engineer, you will be responsible for analyzing, modeling, and optimizing aircraft and propulsion system performance across the full flight envelope. This position requires strong expertise in gas turbine engine cycles, aerodynamics, and thermodynamics to support aircraft development, certification, and operational excellence.

title: Platform Avionics Technician
description_text: We are currently seeking a motivated Platform Avionics Technician to join the Mission Systems Engineering team at Hermeus!

title: Powertrain Responsible Engineer
description_text: As a Powertrain Responsible Engineer you will own the mechanical components and assemblies that generate and transmit power in all forms (fluid, mechanical and electrical) throughout our hypersonic vehicles. This includes gear boxes, generators, motors, and turbomachinery. This highly interdisciplinary role requires strong 1st principles in mechanics, fluid dynamics, thermal analysis, materials science, and manufacturing. You will need to be able to communicate requirements clearly and concisely in a rapidly changing environment while driving the development of cutting-edge hardware that will accelerate the future of hypersonic travel.

title: Principal Operational Analysis and Modeling Engineer
description_text: About The Role: We are currently seeking a creative and dynamic professional to build and lead operational analysis and simulation at Hermeus. As head of operational modeling and simulation, your work will directly shape the series of hypersonic aircraft Hermeus is developing as well as the capabilities they will bring to solve some of the most pressing challenges our customers face.  This role requires government, defense industry, and/or FFRDC/UARC experience and mastery of relevant software.  This role will be filled at a level commensurate with the applicant’s qualifications and experience either at the manager or director level.

title: Propulsion Component Responsible Engineer
description_text: As a Propulsion Component Responsible Engineer you will own the development of flight-critical mechanical and fluid system components, including valves, actuators, and integrated propulsion hardware. You will be responsible for taking hardware from clean-sheet design through build, test, qualification, and production, working in close collaboration with propulsion and vehicle teams. Your work will directly support systems operating in high-pressure, high-temperature, and dynamic flight environments.

title: Propulsion Development Engineer
description_text: As a Development Engineer on the Propulsion team, you will be responsible for advancing engine hardware through rapid, test-driven iteration. You will operate at the intersection of hardware execution and data analysis, owning engine test campaigns and translating results into actionable engineering improvements. This role requires strong hands-on capability, analytical rigor, and the ability to move quickly between test operations, data interpretation, and hardware problem-solving. You will play a critical role in increasing engine development velocity and reliability.

title: Propulsion Engineer Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: As a Propulsion Engineering Intern, you will support the design, development, and testing of propulsion components and systems. You will work closely with engineers, manufacturing teams, and vendors to help bring propulsion hardware from concept through testing.   Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Propulsion Integration Technician
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.   As a Propulsion Integration Technician supporting early-stage R&D and product development, you’ll work at the forefront of propulsion innovation-building, testing, and refining systems that will define future flight hardware. You’ll partner directly with engineers to translate design intent into reality, providing critical feedback on manufacturability, performance, and test readiness. This role requires deep technical skill, adaptability, and hands-on experience developing spaceflight or propulsion systems in fast-paced prototype environments.

title: Propulsion Test Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: At Hermeus, we are constantly iterating and integrating. We test early and often to minimize technical and program risk. We build the test campaigns that support our full range of test programs spanning development, operational and certification testing.   As a Propulsion Test Engineering Intern, you will gain hands-on experience working with high-performance propulsion systems, supporting engine and system-level testing. You will work closely with engineers and technicians to set up test campaigns, monitor data, and troubleshoot issues, directly contributing to Hermeus’ hypersonic development efforts.   Hermeus is seeking talented students to join our Propulsion Test team in Jacksonville for the Spring & Summer 2026 semesters.   Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Quality Inspector
description_text: We are seeking a Quality Inspector to join our growing team and ensure the highest level of quality and reliability across all hardware produced at our facility. As one of the first inspectors on-site, you will play a key role in establishing inspection standards, methods, and processes to support the production of precision aerospace and defense components.    This position requires a highly detail-oriented, hands-on professional with a deep understanding of mechanical inspection, precision measurement, and documentation practices. You’ll work directly with manufacturing, quality engineering, and design teams to ensure all parts meet dimensional and process conformance requirements — from prototype through production.

title: Senior Avionics Electrical Engineer
description_text: As Senior Avionics Electrical Engineer at Hermeus, you will be responsible for the design, testing, and integration of GNC systems, electrical hardware, control software, power distribution, and RF communications for our hypersonic vehicles.

title: Senior Avionics Electrical Engineer
description_text: As Senior Avionics Electrical Engineer at Hermeus, you will be responsible for the design, testing, and integration of GNC systems, electrical hardware, control software, power distribution, and RF communications for our hypersonic vehicles.

title: Senior Avionics Manufacturing Engineer
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.

title: Senior Electrical Engineer - Power Electronics
description_text: As a Senior Power Electronics Electrical Engineer you will be responsible for development of electrical power architectures and components for next-generation supersonic and hypersonic aircraft. This role involves end-to-end ownership of flight hardware and ground support systems while collaborating closely with cross-functional mechanical and electrical engineering teams. You will help bridge the gap between electrical and mechanical disciplines, mentor junior engineers, and contribute to establishing engineering standards and tools across the organization. The position offers a highly hands-on, fast-paced environment focused on rapid prototyping, iterative testing, and solving complex technical challenges that advance hypersonic commercial transport.

title: Senior Enterprise IT Engineer
description_text: About The Role: We’re hiring a hands-on Senior Enterprise IT Engineer who thrives in fast-moving environments and isn’t afraid to get deep into the weeds. You’ll be building, fixing, automating, and securing critical IT, ICS, and IoT systems that keep the business moving. This is a high-impact role where you’ll help define and scale the infrastructure that powers our growing company.

title: Senior Flight Test Engineer
description_text: About The Role: As a Senior Flight Test Engineer at Hermeus, you will help plan, coordinate, and execute ground and flight test campaigns for advanced high-speed aircraft systems. This role sits at the center of flight operations, systems integration, mission planning, and test execution, working closely with engineering, certification, operations, and flight crews to ensure vehicles are safely configured and ready to meet program objectives. You will support everything from test planning and procedure development to test card generation, real-time execution, debriefs, data analysis, and reporting. This is a hands-on role for someone who thrives in fast-moving test environments, can solve problems in real time, and brings strong judgment, technical rigor, and a deep commitment to safety.

title: Senior GNC Engineer - Flight Controls & Autonomy
description_text: About The Role: As a Senior GNC Engineer at Hermeus, you will help develop and deploy the flight control, navigation, and autonomy systems that enable our aircraft to fly safely and perform reliably in demanding real-world environments. This role will focus heavily on flight controls development, controls modeling, state estimation, and autonomy for advanced aircraft applications, including autonomous takeoff and landing.   You will work across simulation, software, hardware, and flight test to build and validate advanced control and estimation capabilities for high-speed aircraft. This role is ideal for an engineer who enjoys solving hard dynamic systems problems, moving quickly from analysis to implementation, and working closely with cross-functional teams to take algorithms from concept through validation and flight.

title: Senior GNC Engineer - Navigation & System Integration
description_text: About The Role: The Navigation and System Integration GNC Engineer will be responsible for integration, testing, validation and verification of Navigation instrumentation and GNSS components and supporting the navigation SW suite and development efforts (including filtering and fusion) on advanced guidance, navigation and control systems for our aircraft, as needed.

title: Senior Manager Capital Projects
description_text: About the role:    As the Senior Manager or Head of Capital Projects, you will lead the planning and execution of complex capital projects, overseeing the full lifecycle from initial scoping through construction and final handoff. You will partner with cross-functional stakeholders to define project requirements, budgets, and timelines, while managing architects, engineers, contractors, and vendors to ensure projects are delivered on schedule, within budget, and to the highest quality standards. This role is responsible for driving permitting and regulatory compliance, maintaining detailed project schedules and financial tracking in collaboration with Procurement and Finance, and proactively managing risks, changes, and contractor performance. You will serve as a key liaison between leadership and project teams, providing clear reporting and ensuring all projects transition seamlessly to Facilities and Workplace Operations upon completion.

title: Senior Manager of Operational Test
description_text: Aboout this Role: As a Senior Manager of Operational Test,  you will lead the planning, coordination, and execution of complex flight test and mission operations supporting the development of the world’s fastest aircraft . You will serve as the operational bridge between engineering, flight test, safety, and ground operations teams to ensure missions are executed safely, efficiently, and in alignment with program objectives   You will oversee payload integration, mission planning, operational readiness, and execution across flight test campaigns and deployed operations. The ideal candidate brings strong operational leadership, experience in aviation or aerospace mission environments, and the ability to coordinate complex activities in fast-paced, high-consequence environments.   A  commercial or military pilot license is highly valued , as pilot perspective plays an important role in mission planning, range coordination, test card development, and operational decision-making.   Work location and hybrid status are negotiable.

title: Senior Manager, Corporate & Legal Operations
description_text: About the role: As the Senior Manager, Corporate & Legal Operations you will be a key member of the legal team, responsible for strategically implementing day-to-day legal operations, managing workflows, and driving efficiency across the department and cross-functional processes. This role will serve as the operational backbone of the legal department, responsible for building the systems, workflows, and infrastructure that allow the legal team to scale with the Company’s rapid growth. This role combines substantive paralegal work with legal operations responsibilities, requiring someone who can move fluidly between hands-on legal support and process improvement. The position includes direct involvement in corporate finance transactions, supporting debt financing and equity raise processes from document management through closing.

title: Senior Mechanisms Engineer
description_text: Hermeus is seeking a Senior Mechanisms Engineer to join our Vehicle & Mechanisms Team. In this senior-level role, you will be responsible for designing and developing complex mechanical systems and mechanisms that are critical to the success of our high-speed aircraft projects.

title: Senior Operations and Test Engineer
description_text: About The Role: As a Senior Operations and Test Engineer at Hermeus, you will help develop, test, and operate advanced aircraft systems through hands-on integration, checkout, development testing, and taxi test campaigns. This role blends engineering, operations, and automation, with a focus on building standardized processes and tools that make vehicle testing safer, faster, and more repeatable. You will work closely with technicians, specialists, and cross-functional engineering teams to support hardware integration, troubleshoot off-nominal results, improve test readiness, and turn lessons learned into better procedures, equipment, and workflows. This is a fast-paced, hands-on role for someone who is comfortable working around complex and hazardous systems, enjoys solving problems in real time, and can help drive reliable operations in a rapid hardware development environment.

title: Senior Propulsion Test Engineer
description_text: About this Role: As a Senior Propulsion Test Engineer, you will support the development, integration, and operation of propulsion test systems at our Florida facility. This is a hands-on, high-impact role focused on building test capabilities and executing test campaigns for next-generation hypersonic propulsion systems.    You will work closely with engineers and technicians across multiple disciplines to design test hardware, operate complex test facilities, and ensure safe, reliable, and repeatable performance. This role is ideal for an engineer who enjoys building, troubleshooting, and operating large-scale test systems and wants to be directly involved in advancing high-Mach technology.

title: Senior RF Systems Engineer
description_text: As an RF systems engineer you will be responsible for identifying RF hardware requirements, creating system architectures, and building/testing/integrating the RF solutions – both on the aircraft and ground station. This position will require strong fundamentals, wide-breadth design experience, and efficient troubleshooting skills. You’ll be responsible for high-ownership technical delivery while working with a multi-disciplinary team of engineering, manufacturing, and quality professionals.

title: Senior RF Systems Engineer
description_text: As an RF systems engineer you will be responsible for identifying RF hardware requirements, creating system architectures, and building/testing/integrating the RF solutions – both on the aircraft and ground station. This position will require strong fundamentals, wide-breadth design experience, and efficient troubleshooting skills. You’ll be responsible for high-ownership technical delivery while working with a multi-disciplinary team of engineering, manufacturing, and quality professionals.

title: Senior Software Simulation Engineer
Senior Strategic Sourcing Manager, Indirect
description_text: About The Role: We are seeking a Senior Strategic Sourcing Manager, Indirect to lead sourcing strategy and supplier management across critical indirect spend categories within a fast-paced aerospace and defense environment. This is a highly strategic role focused on building scalable sourcing frameworks, driving supplier performance, and supporting rapid company growth from development through production scale-up. You will own end-to-end sourcing activities across categories such as Professional Services, Software/SaaS, IT Infrastructure, Facilities, Capital Equipment, and Corporate Services. The ideal candidate is highly autonomous, commercially strong, operationally minded, and comfortable operating in ambiguity while partnering cross-functionally with Engineering, Manufacturing, Finance, Legal, IT, and Executive Leadership.

title: Senior Structures Engineer
description_text: About The Role: This role is primarily responsible for the design, build and test of next generation aircraft aerostructures including fuselage, empennage, control surfaces and landing gear. We are currently in the process of designing iterative airframes on the path to hypersonic flight and looking for a Senior Structures Engineer to lead hardware upgrades, clean sheet designs and mentor junior engineers here in Atlanta.

title: Senior Structures Engineer
description_text: This role is primarily responsible for the design, build and test of next generation aircraft aerostructures including fuselage, empennage, control surfaces and landing gear. We are currently in the process of designing iterative airframes on the path to hypersonic flight and looking for a Senior Structures Engineer to lead hardware upgrades, clean sheet designs and mentor junior engineers here in Atlanta.

title: Senior Welding Engineer
description_text: We are open to Senior or Principal leveling for this role. This position will support manufacturing and development of Hermeus vehicles and will help stand up welding processes, develop, qualify, and optimize welding designs and processes including but not limited to laser beam welding, gas tungsten arc welding, resistance welding, gas metal arc welding, and plasma arc welding. The welding engineer in this role will interface daily with design and manufacturing engineers to make welding judgment calls and improvements and will enforce standardized practices and procedures on multiple welded assemblies.

title: Sheet Metal Shop Technician
description_text: We are seeking a Sheet Metal Shop Technician to support precision fabrication of complex, tight-tolerance flight hardware. In this role, you will set up, operate, and maintain advanced sheet metal cutting and forming equipment to produce components that meet rigorous aerospace-quality standards.    This is a hands-on, high-impact position where craftsmanship, attention to detail, and process discipline are essential. As a senior member of the fabrication team, you’ll take ownership of your equipment, mentor junior operators, and collaborate closely with manufacturing and engineering to continuously improve fabrication methods and product quality.

title: Software Engineer - Mission Systems
description_text: The Mission Systems Engineering (MSE) Team develops the Mission Management System (MMS)—a software platform that integrates mission subsystems, autonomy services, operator interfaces, communications links, and telemetry for Hermeus aircraft. The MMS enables rapid incorporation of new capabilities from internal teams and third-party providers. As a Software Engineer on this team, you will design, implement, and test C++ software that extends this platform and interfaces with mission subsystems, autonomy solutions, and external services.

title: Software Engineer HILs
description_text: About The Role: We are seeking a multidisciplinary engineer that will help us develop our next generation Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testbeds for our hypersonic aircraft. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining HIL testbeds that will validate complex avionics systems. You will work closely with software, hardware, and systems engineers to ensure product reliability, functionality, and compliance with specifications.  We are open to hiring a fresh graduate with experience working on a highly integrated school project/team like Cubesat, Rocketry, Drone Racing or similar.

title: Software Engineer HILs
description_text: About The Role: We are seeking a multidisciplinary engineer that will help us develop our next generation Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testbeds for our hypersonic aircraft. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining HIL testbeds that will validate complex avionics systems. You will work closely with software, hardware, and systems engineers to ensure product reliability, functionality, and compliance with specifications.    We are open to hiring a fresh graduate with experience working on a highly integrated school project/team like Cubesat, Rocketry, Drone Racing or similar.

title: Software Engineering Intern (HIL) - Fall 2026
description_text: The Flight Software "Hardware in the Loop" intern will support the setup, integration, and testing of real-time simulation environments that connect hardware components with software models.    Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Software Engineering Intern (HMI) - Fall 2026
description_text: Join our team developing cutting-edge software for the Quarterhorse supersonic unmanned aircraft. You'll work with C++ and web technologies (TypeScript/JavaScript) to build control systems for both remotely piloted (HOTAS) and autonomous flight modes. This role includes designing and implementing intuitive user interfaces, ranging from hands-on control to point-and-click autonomy management.

title: Software Engineering Intern (Modeling & Simulation) - Fall 2026
description_text: As a Modeling & Simulation Software Engineering Intern you will work with the software team to develop performant modeling and simulation code for our 6DOF SITL simulation using Julia. You'll Interface with the Flight Software, HMI, and Flight Sciences teams to meet stakeholder needs and ensure accurate results. Your work will involve creating hardware/physics informed software models and validate results with real data.    Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Spike Inlet Mechanisms Build Engineer
description_text: The Spike Inlet Mechanism Build Engineer will be responsible for the build, integration, and test of supersonic spike inlet mechanisms and mechanical subsystems. This role bridges design, manufacturing, and test to ensure mechanism hardware is manufacturable, reliable, and performant. The engineer will work closely with design engineers, technicians, manufacturing teams, and test engineers to develop hardware, improve build processes, and support delivering rapid hardware iterations in a fast-paced development environment.

title: Strategic Finance Analyst
description_text: We are seeking a Strategic Finance Analyst to join our growing finance team in a high-impact role that bridges operational support and long-term planning. The ideal candidate is intellectually curious, competitive, and excited by the opportunity to be the first person to tackle a problem. You should have the flexibility to partner with teams from across the business, such as translating engineering and supply-chain challenges into quantitative performance measures and financial solutions. Foremost, you should be energized to support a defense aviation company that is redefining the pace of hardware R&D in the 21 st century. RESPONSIBILITIES: Own end-to-end financial models for products and business lines, with responsibility to gather and sanitize data across the business Develop novel financial frameworks to support strategic decisions across manufacturing, supply chain, facilities, and other functions Execute rapid, scrappy, “80% fidelity” analyses on short timelines that later feed into scalable, long-term solutions Create transparency into manufacturing costs alongside FP&A, and explore cost reduction roadmaps for major initiatives Maintain competitive landscape databases and target screening to identify emerging opportunities and threats Support investor relations functions , including drafting of executive & board level materials Partner with Advanced Development, Supply Chain, Manufacturing, and Growth functions to provide financial support for multi-year endeavors and proposals   REQUIREMENTS: 1-3 years of experience in one or more of the following: corporate finance, investment banking, FP&A, consulting, or relevant finance roles at a high-growth hardware or manufacturing startup Bachelor’s degree from an accredited US institution, Finance, Economics, Accounting, or related field preferred Strong preference for experience or demonstrated interest in aerospace and defense industry, government contracting, or other highly regulated field Advanced proficiency in Excel, including dynamic financial models and scenario analyses Willingness to learn and engage with corporate ERP & FP&A systems (Netsuite, Manufact u ro, Adaptive) Demonstrated ability to thrive in a past-paced, dynamic startup environment Self-starter comfortable working in a hybrid work environment, where many teams members will be located in cross-continent settings Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage with customers, internal engineer & test teams, and suppliers effectively U.S. Citizen or lawful permanent resident status

title: Structures Design Engineer
description_text: As a Structures Design Engineer, you will be responsible for the design, analysis, and delivery of primary and secondary aircraft structures for hypersonic vehicles. You will own hardware from early trade studies through detailed design, manufacturing, integration, and flight test, working closely with propulsion, aerothermal, manufacturing, and test teams.  This role requires strong fundamentals in structural design, comfort operating in ambiguous problem spaces, and the ability to move quickly while maintaining engineering discipline.

title: Structures Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: This role sits at the intersection of engineering design, manufacturing, integration and test for flight vehicles. You'll be embedded with design and production teams, owning everything from hardware design, tooling, testing and flight readiness to process development and vehicle assembly.   Summer: Around 12 weeks (May - August) Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Structures Integration Technician
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.   As a Structures Integration Technician, you will play a critical role in the hands-on assembly, integration, and testing of flight and ground hardware. This role requires someone equally comfortable working across structures, fluids, inventory, and manufacturing tasks, as needed. You’ll be expected to bring deep technical expertise, excellent judgment, and a self-starting attitude to a highly collaborative team in a fast-paced, low-oversight environment.  We’re seeking someone who’s open-minded, adaptive, and eager to contribute across domains to help bring complex systems from concept to reality.

title: Structures Manufacturing Engineer
description_text: As a Structures Manufacturing Engineer – Tooling & R&D at Hermeus, you will enable the rapid development, integration, and test of flight-critical structural assemblies through innovative tooling and hands-on manufacturing execution. This role is deeply embedded in early-stage design-build-test cycles , where speed, creativity, and engineering judgment matter as much as formal process.   You will design and build tooling, fixtures, and ground support equipment while partnering closely with structures, test, and manufacturing teams to turn evolving designs into real, testable hardware. This is a highly hands-on role for engineers who like being on the floor, solving problems in real time, and iterating quickly to move flight hardware forward.

title: Structures Manufacturing Engineer
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.

title: Subsystem Test Engineering Intern - Fall 2026
description_text: The Test team is responsible for developing infrastructure to perform tests on fuel & hydraulic system components. The test team works with hardware owners to define testing requirements, design & implement test fixtures, and assist in data collection to inform and verify design decisions.   Structures Test - ATL  Subsystem Test - LA    Fall: Around 16 weeks (August - December)

title: Teamcenter Systems Administrator
description_text: About The Role: As a Teamcenter Systems Administrator, you will support and scale our Siemens Teamcenter environment across Engineering and Manufacturing operations. This is a highly operational, user-facing role focused on Teamcenter administration, engineering workflow support, release management, troubleshooting, engineering data integrity, and continuous process improvement. While the role will support Siemens NX users and CAD-related workflows, the primary focus is Teamcenter operations and enabling efficient engineering data management across the organization.       You will work directly with Engineering, Manufacturing, Quality, Supply Chain, and Digital Operations teams to ensure Teamcenter remains reliable, scalable, and operationally effective as the company continues to grow. This is not a passive ticket-based support role. The ideal candidate is comfortable troubleshooting complex operational issues, observing real user behavior, improving workflows, supporting releases, administering system configurations, and proactively identifying opportunities to reduce engineering friction and improving processes.

title: Technical Recruiter
description_text: This is a 6-month contract role with the potential to convert to full-time. The team is directly responsible for bringing in the people who shape the company’s growth and execution.   Recruiting at Hermeus means working on technically challenging, high-impact roles in an environment where hiring quality and speed genuinely matter. You’ll be exposed to complex searches across multiple disciplines and collaborate closely with leadership as the company scales.

title: Test Engineer
description_text: About The Role: As a Test Engineer at Hermeus, you will play a pivotal role in developing, building, analyzing and operating test assets for our hypersonic vehicles. This position offers a unique opportunity to work on groundbreaking technology in a fast-paced and collaborative environment. As part of this team, you will have the opportunity to work on a broad range of vehicle subsystems including hydraulic system, fuel system, mechanisms, high-voltage, mechanisms, airframe structural, and more!

title: Test Engineer
description_text: About The Role: As a Test Engineer at Hermeus, you will play a pivotal role in developing, building, analyzing and operating test assets for our hypersonic vehicles. This position offers a unique opportunity to work on groundbreaking technology in a fast-paced and collaborative environment. As part of this team, you will have the opportunity to work on a broad range of vehicle subsystems including hydraulic system, fuel system, mechanisms, high-voltage, mechanisms, airframe structural, and more!

title: Test Technician / Specialist
description_text: About The Role: As a Test Specialist at Hermeus you will have the opportunity to build and maintain test assets and infrastructure as well as execute test campaigns that are critical to Hermeus’s core development approach of rapid, iterative testing. This position offers a unique opportunity for growth and development while working on groundbreaking technology in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. As part of this team, you will have the opportunity to work on a broad range of vehicle subsystems including hydraulic system, fuel system, mechanisms, high-voltage, mechanisms, airframe structural, and more!

title: Test Technician / Specialist
description_text: About The Role: As a Test Specialist at Hermeus you will have the opportunity to build and maintain test assets and infrastructure as well as execute test campaigns that are critical to Hermeus’s core development approach of rapid, iterative testing. This position offers a unique opportunity for growth and development while working on groundbreaking technology in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. As part of this team, you will have the opportunity to work on a broad range of vehicle subsystems including hydraulic system, fuel system, mechanisms, high-voltage, mechanisms, airframe structural, and more!

title: Tooling Engineer
description_text: A tooling engineer on the structural engineering team is responsible for the design, analysis, fabrication, and use of various drill tools, jigs, integration fixtures, and ground support equipment in support of major structural assemblies.

title: Tooling Engineer
description_text: A Tooling Build Engineer on the Manufacturing team is responsible for the design, development, and validation of innovative tooling, fixtures, and ground support equipment that enable the prototyping, integration, and testing of advanced structural assemblies. This role emphasizes creativity, rapid iteration, and collaboration with multidisciplinary teams to support early-stage research and development.

title: Vehicle Integration Technician
description_text: The Manufacturing Team at Hermeus are the boots on the ground. We have a vast array of experiences ranging from welding, additive, tube bending, machining, integration, and avionics. We are a lean team who are responsible for building a vehicle that will fly. We provide crucial design feedback to our engineering team and can rapidly iterate on design and assembly.   The Vehicle Integration Technician is responsible for the integration of all secondary structure, avionics, and propulsion components and systems to the vehicle’s primary structure. The task requires both avionics and mechanical integration skills along with pneumatic and avionics checkout activity.

title: Welder
description_text: About The Role: As a Welder , you will play a key role in fabricating, assembling, and joining structural and piping components critical to our production and mission operations. You’ll work closely with engineering and manufacturing teams to execute precise, high-quality welds across a variety of materials, ensuring all products meet rigorous performance and safety standards. This role is hands-on and suited for an experienced professional who thrives in a fast-paced, technical, and safety-focused environment.

title: Welding Manufacturing Engineer
description_text: As a Welding Manufacturing Engineer , you will play a critical role in enabling rapid development, fabrication, and integration of advanced hardware across our R&D and prototype manufacturing environments. You’ll be responsible for developing weld processes, fixtures, and joint designs that bridge early design concepts to flight-ready hardware. This position is hands-on and deeply cross-functional, working directly with design, manufacturing, and test teams to bring complex systems from concept to reality.

title: Flight Test Engineer
description_text: The Flight Test Engineer at Elroy Air will drive the development and certification of Chaparral, our autonomous VTOL cargo aircraft reshaping middle-mile logistics. You'll architect and execute flight test programs that push the boundaries of autonomous flight systems, working with engineers, pilots, and technicians to validate performance in real-world operations. This role offers the chance to tackle cutting-edge challenges in vertical flight and autonomy while directly impacting how critical supplies reach communities worldwide.

title: Mechanical Engineer - Mechanical Systems
description_text: As a Mechanical Engineer you will be responsible for designing, analyzing, building, and testing the flight-critical mechanical systems that bring our autonomous cargo UAV to life. You will work on a range of mechanical and structural systems, driving mechanical design and decision making that enables aircraft testing and flight.

title: Senior Structural Analyst
description_text: As a Senior Structural Analyst on the Mechanical Engineering team you will be responsible for performing comprehensive structural analysis supporting exciting new products throughout the entire product lifecycle. In this role, you will help define requirements, guide concepts, perform trade studies, optimize preliminary designs, check detailed designs, and support structural test campaigns and certification. Working as part of a small team to develop validated methods, tools, and templates, you will help set the standard (and perhaps the state-of-the-art) for aircraft structural analysis. This is an on-site, full-time, exempt role based at our headquarters in South San Francisco, CA.
